title: Elegant Crispy British Seafood Tart
description: A fresh, crispy brunch-mood British tart starring a simmered frozen seafood mix. It is baked in a vegan shortcrust and finished with dill.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200 degrees Celsius.
In a large bowl combine 100g all-purpose flour, 50g vegan butter and 1 tsp sea salt and rub together until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add 2 tbsp chilled water, mixing until a dough forms.
Divide the dough into 4 portions, roll each to 3mm thickness and cut out circles that fit 6cm tart tins.
Prick each dough circle with a fork, then bake in the preheated oven for 10 minutes until lightly golden.
While the crusts bake, thaw 200g frozen seafood mix and drain any excess water.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat and simmer the seafood with the juice of 1 lemon for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Spoon the hot seafood mixture into the cooled crusts, leaving a 1cm border around the edge.
Sprinkle 1 tbsp chopped fresh dill over the top of each tart.
Return the filled tins to the oven and bake for an additional 12 minutes until the crust is crisp and the filling is heated through.
